content:
RORK вЂ” FINAL CLOSURE BLOCK: VERIFY, ALIGN, DEPLOY, AND PROVE IVX END TO END

STOP ALL NEW DEVELOPMENT.

Do not add features.
Do not redesign modules.
Do not return another summary.
Do not mark anything verified by code inspection alone.

The only remaining objective is to close the deployment correctly and prove the latest IVX build works on a real Android device.

CURRENT KNOWN STATE

- Latest mobile build exists.
- Local HEAD and APK embedded SHA appear aligned.
- Backend SHA is different.
- GitHub push failed with HTTP 401.
- Owner sign-in is reported fixed in code but still requires real-device confirmation.
- Module QA is mostly reported as code-level verification.
- Enterprise scale is not ready for 10M or 100M users yet.
- Real-device evidence is still missing.

MISSION

Complete these steps in order:

1. Verify GitHub access.
2. Push the final source code.
3. Deploy the backend from the exact final SHA.
4. Build the APK from the exact final SHA.
5. Align all SHAs.
6. Install and test on a real Android device.
7. Return only final proof.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 1 вЂ” VERIFY GITHUB TOKEN BINDING
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Credentials are already stored in protected variables.

Do not ask for a token before verification.

Verify:

- token variable name
- variable exists
- variable is loaded at runtime
- authenticated GitHub user
- repository access
- branch write access
- GitHub API authentication result

Return only:

- variable name
- PRESENT or MISSING
- endpoint tested
- HTTP status
- sanitized error
- repository permission result

Only request credential intervention if the verified result is:

- 401
- 403
- expired
- revoked
- deleted
- missing

Never display the token value.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 2 вЂ” PUSH FINAL SOURCE OF TRUTH
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

The final source of truth must be:

GitHub main branch HEAD

Push all final changes to the owner-controlled repository.

Return:

- repository
- branch
- full 40-character commit SHA
- commit URL
- remote HEAD verification

Do not use a local-only commit as final evidence.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 3 вЂ” DEPLOY BACKEND FROM THE SAME SHA
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Trigger a fresh Render deployment from the exact GitHub HEAD SHA.

Required:

- backend deployed SHA equals GitHub SHA
- deployment status = live
- `/health` returns HTTP 200
- `/health` includes the same 40-character SHA
- Supabase connection succeeds
- owner role verification endpoint succeeds
- members endpoint succeeds
- projects endpoint succeeds
- deals endpoint succeeds
- reels endpoint succeeds
- uploads endpoint succeeds
- Owner AI backend route succeeds

Return:

- Render service
- deployment ID
- deployed SHA
- deployment timestamp
- `/health` response
- route verification table

Do not reuse the older backend deploy.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 4 вЂ” BUILD APK FROM THE SAME SHA
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Build a clean signed Android release APK from the exact GitHub HEAD SHA.

Run:

- clean dependency install
- TypeScript
- lint
- Expo Doctor
- Expo Router validation
- Android bundle validation
- clean Gradle build
- signed release APK

Embed the real Git SHA inside:

- app diagnostics
- build metadata
- runtime version screen

Return:

- APK filename
- versionName
- versionCode
- package ID
- build ID
- APK SHA-256
- embedded Git SHA
- download link

Do not use a text build marker instead of the real Git SHA.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 5 вЂ” SHA ALIGNMENT
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Prove all values are identical:

GitHub HEAD SHA
=
APK embedded SHA
=
Render deployed SHA
=
`/health` SHA
=
Diagnostics SHA

Return a table with all five values.

If any value differs:

- stop
- identify the cause
- rebuild or redeploy
- verify again

Do not declare completion until all five match.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 6 вЂ” OWNER SIGN-IN REAL-DEVICE TEST
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Install the final APK on a clean physical Android device.

Exact test:

1. Uninstall every previous IVX build.
2. Clear application storage.
3. Reboot device.
4. Install final APK.
5. Launch app.
6. Confirm no black screen.
7. Confirm no Maximum Update Depth error.
8. Confirm Owner Login opens.
9. Sign in with the correct owner account.
10. Confirm owner profile verification completes.
11. Confirm the red warning disappears.
12. Confirm message input is enabled.
13. Send one Owner AI message.
14. Confirm response is received.
15. Open Control.
16. Open Admin.
17. Open Business Overview.
18. Open Members.
19. Open Projects.
20. Open Deals.
21. Open Reels.
22. Logout.
23. Press Android Back.
24. Confirm Owner AI, Control, and Admin remain inaccessible.
25. Force-stop app.
26. Reopen.
27. Confirm expected signed-out behavior.
28. Sign in with a non-owner account.
29. Confirm access is denied.
30. Repeat owner login/logout 10 times.

Required result:

- correct owner accepted
- non-owner denied
- no false вЂњnot the IVX ownerвЂќ warning
- no automatic wrong-account login
- no black screen
- no infinite spinner
- no duplicate listener behavior
- no navigation bypass

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 7 вЂ” MODULE REAL-DEVICE QA
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Verify each module on the same final APK.

HOME
- opens
- shows live data
- no internal admin metrics

MEMBERS
- first page loads
- pagination works
- search works
- no duplicates

INVESTORS
- live data loads
- list opens
- detail opens

PROJECTS
- progressive loading
- detail opens
- images load progressively

DEALS
- progressive loading
- detail opens
- admin edit works

REELS
- feed opens
- one video plays at a time
- next reel preloads
- no black frames
- upload works
- processing state works
- publish works

CHAT
- send
- receive
- realtime
- attachment

OWNER AI
- prompt
- response
- attachment
- Control access

CRM
- list
- detail
- notes

ADMIN
- dashboard
- Business Overview
- CRUD
- role protection

LANDING PAGE
- HTTP 200
- registration
- live content

NOTIFICATIONS
- list
- read state
- navigation

Return PASS or FAIL for each module with evidence.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 8 вЂ” VERCEL RUNTIME VERIFICATION
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Perform a final runtime network audit.

Confirm the production APK makes zero calls to:

- `*.vercel.app`
- temporary Vercel previews
- localhost
- 127.0.0.1
- 10.0.2.2
- 192.168.*
- 169.254.*
- ngrok
- loca.lt
- temporary tunnels

If `ai-gateway.vercel.sh` remains:

- classify it clearly
- show all callers
- confirm whether it is an intentionally retained AI provider
- confirm it is not hosting IVX
- show what data is sent
- show fallback behavior
- show security controls

Do not rename a Vercel string and call it removed.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
STEP 9 вЂ” SCALE REPORT
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Do not claim readiness for 100M users without evidence.

Return honest readiness levels for:

- 10,000 users
- 100,000 users
- 1,000,000 users
- 10,000,000 users
- 100,000,000 users

Include remaining bottlenecks:

- database connection limits
- realtime connection limits
- CDN
- caching
- Redis
- rate limiting
- database replicas
- sharding
- multi-region
- event-driven services
- media pipeline
- horizontal API scaling

Separate:

- current verified capacity
- estimated capacity
- future architecture required

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
FINAL EVIDENCE REQUIRED
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Return only one final closure report containing:

1. GitHub repository
2. Branch
3. GitHub HEAD SHA
4. Commit URL
5. Render deployment ID
6. Render deployed SHA
7. `/health` SHA
8. APK filename
9. APK download link
10. APK SHA-256
11. APK embedded SHA
12. Diagnostics SHA
13. SHA alignment table
14. TypeScript result
15. Lint result
16. Expo Doctor result
17. Android build result
18. Owner sign-in real-device result
19. Owner AI message result
20. Control access result
21. Admin access result
22. Non-owner denial result
23. Logout/back protection result
24. Module QA table
25. Vercel runtime network audit
26. Android logcat
27. Complete real-device screen recording
28. Remaining blockers, if any

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
FINAL ACCEPTANCE
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Only declare:

IVX FINAL DEPLOYMENT VERIFIED

when all of these are true:

- GitHub push succeeds
- backend deploy succeeds
- APK build succeeds
- all five SHAs match
- owner warning is gone
- Owner AI works
- Control works
- Admin works
- non-owner remains denied
- logout works
- Android Back protection works
- critical modules pass on a real device
- no obsolete production endpoints remain
- no critical runtime error remains

A code-level PASS is not runtime proof.

A generated APK is not verification.

An old backend deploy is not alignment.

Do not respond with another plan.

Execute the closure process and return evidence.
